metric_value: with consumer and corporate notebook computers 'flying off the shelves,' the mobile segment of the PC industry has become the fastest growing -- prompting PC producers, chipmakers and software vendors to focus on the market.

metric_name: Desktop share of corporate primary devices
metric_value: by 2006, just 45 percent of corporate users will be primarily dependent on a desktop machine

metric_name: Notebook+tablet share of corporate primary devices
metric_value: 40 percent of users will count a notebook or tablet PC as their primary device
15 percent will be using thin clients or other information appliances, such as handhelds
notes: Meta Group forecast — overshot slightly; thin clients stayed niche

metric_name: Average user interacts with 4+ computing devices
metric_value: By 2007, the average user will interact regularly with at least four distinct computing devices -- a personal home PC, smart digital entertainment system, corporate computer and mobile information device
notes: Kleynhans quote — validated and exceeded; 2024 avg is 6+

metric_value: This multiplicity of devices will force software vendors to focus on information synchronization as well as 'thinning' or 'roaming' applications that enable users to access their information independent of the device they are using.
notes: Kleynhans prediction; validated by iCloud (2011), OneDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive

metric_name: US portable share of PC shipments
metric_value: In the U.S., we're forecasting portables to compose 47 percent of all PC shipments in 2007
notes: Promisel IDC forecast — notebooks actually hit ~50% US by 2008

metric_name: Record Q4 2003 PC shipments led by notebooks
metric_value: worldwide PC shipments hit record numbers last quarter, driven by notebooks

metric_name: Tablet form factor to go mainstream by 2006
metric_value: trends will help move the tablet form factor into the mainstream by 2006, when one-third of all corporate notebooks will include tablet capabilities.
notes: Meta Group forecast — incorrect; tablet PC languished until iPad-driven convertibles emerged 2012-2015

metric_value: Global notebook shipments exceeded desktop shipments for the first time in 2008 per IDC data (Q3 2008 was inflection quarter)
By 2010, notebooks reached 70%+ of all PC shipments globally per IDC/Gartner tracking
notes: Meta Group 2004 prediction exceeded on pace
